questions.question: What was ViT's radical simplification of computer vision?
options: Deeper convolutional stacks

Drop convolutions entirely: slice the image into fixed patches, linearly project each into an embedding, and feed the sequence to a VANILLA transformer encoder — at sufficient pretraining scale it matches or beats ResNets
Hand-crafted SIFT features revived
Trees of binary classifiers
questions.explanation: "An Image is Worth 16x16 Words" (Dosovitskiy 2020). The pedagogical punchline: no vision-specific layers at all — the encoder is identical to BERT's.

questions.question: What does the standard ViT patchify step produce?
options: One token for the whole image
A 224×224 image cut into 16×16 patches gives a 14×14 grid = 196 tokens of 768 values each — patch size is the lever: smaller patches mean more tokens and better resolution at quadratic attention cost
A 50,000-token pixel sequence
An edge map fed to an RNN
questions.explanation: The linear embedding is literally nn.Conv2d(C, d_model, kernel_size=P, stride=P) — a 2-line implementation that turns vision into sequence modeling.

questions.question: What is the [CLS] token's role, and when is it discarded?
options: It marks the image's top-left corner

A learnable token prepended to the sequence whose final hidden state serves as the whole-image representation for classification — DINOv2 and SAM skip it and use the patch embeddings directly for dense tasks
It stores the class label during training
It is required by all ViT variants
questions.explanation: Classification wants one vector; segmentation and retrieval want per-patch features. The head choice follows the task.

questions.question: What scale condition does ViT need to beat CNNs, and why?
options: None — ViT always wins

Large pretraining (ImageNet-21k or bigger): transformers lack convolution's built-in locality and translation-equivariance priors, so they need more data to learn those regularities — but then scale further
Only TPU hardware
Batch sizes above 65k
questions.explanation: The same weak-prior trade as language transformers vs RNNs. CNNs still win on edge devices and latency-sensitive tasks; everything else has a ViT in the stack by 2026.

questions.question: What broader 2026 pattern did ViT inaugurate?
options: Custom architectures per modality

One architecture, many modalities: Whisper tokenizes audio, ViT tokenizes images, robotics uses action tokens, video uses pixel/patch tokens — the transformer doesn't care, feed it a sequence and it learns
The return of hand-tuned features
Smaller models replacing larger ones
questions.explanation: Tokenize-then-transform became the universal recipe — the conceptual bridge from this phase to the multimodal models of Phase 12.
